articles.title: Why the Sabarimala verdict allowing women into the temple differs from the hijab ‘ban’
articles.summary: The article compares the Supreme Court's verdict allowing women of all ages into the Sabarimala temple with the debate over the hijab ban in Karnataka. It argues that the Sabarimala ruling prioritizes individual autonomy, while the hijab ban is framed around biological differences. Both issues stem from patriarchal and religious influences. The verdict is a legal action with implications for gender rights and welfare policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Both are practices shaped by patriarchy and religion. But one is determined by individual autonomy and the other by biology.

These decisions aim to enhance connectivity and healthcare access in the state.

articles.title: No Right To Permanent Alternate Accommodation For Daughter-In-Law Under DV Act Or Senior Citizens Act: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that neither the Domestic Violence Act, 2005, nor the Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007, contemplates grant of “permanent accommodation” to a daughter-in-law, but onlyrecognizes right of shared-household. Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav was dealing with a plea filed by elderly parents challenging an appellate authority's direction requiring them to provide “permanent alternate accommodation” to their daughter-in-law and grandchildren

articles.title: NCLAT Upholds CIRP Admission as Conditional Payment Email Acknowledged Operational Debt
articles.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) upheld the admission of a Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) after ruling that an email from the Corporate Debtor acknowledging a conditional payment amounted to an acknowledgment of operational debt. The Tribunal dismissed claims of a pre-existing dispute regarding quality and delays as not genuine under Section 9 of the IBC. This decision reinforces the legal framework for operational debt recognition in insolvency proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: NCLAT held that the Corporate Debtor’s email offering payment subject to acceptance of a consequence sheet amounted to acknowledgment of operational debt. The Tribunal found that the dispute raised regarding quality and delays was not a genuine pre-existing dispute under Section 9 of the IBC. The post NCLAT Upholds CIRP Admission as Conditional Payment Email Acknowledged Operational Debt appeared first on TaxGuru .

The ruling impacts healthcare education and service delivery standards in India.

This move aims to reduce pollution and promote cleaner transportation in the region.

articles.title: SC Allows Deduction of Grants as They Were Part of Statutory Business Activity
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that grants disbursed by a statutory corporation qualify as deductible revenue expenditure because they were part of its core business functions. The court clarified that such grants were not merely an application of income. This decision impacts how statutory corporations and tax authorities interpret revenue expenditure for tax purposes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that grants disbursed by a statutory corporation formed part of its core business functions and qualified as deductible revenue expenditure. The ruling clarified that such grants were not mere application of income. The post SC Allows Deduction of Grants as They Were Part of Statutory Business Activity appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: NPCI to roll out one Soundbox for all UPI apps: What it means for retail payments
articles.summary: The National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) is introducing a single Soundbox device that will allow merchants to accept UPI payments from any app, reducing costs for businesses. This move could impact revenue for major UPI players like PhonePe, BharatPe, and Paytm. The initiative aims to enhance interoperability and streamline retail payments in India.
articles.raw_summary: NPCI is building a unified, interoperable Soundbox for merchants, letting them accept UPI payments from any app via one device. articles.title: ITAT Mumbai Quashes Reassessment as Section 148 Notice Was Issued Beyond Surviving Limitation Period

articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is introducing new rules for banks under Basel III. Lenders will soon publish detailed information quarterly on capital, leverage, liquidity, and risks. articles.title: Gauhati HC Refuses Higher Bank Guarantee as Interim GST Relief Was Meant to Secure Tax Amount Only
articles.summary: The Gauhati High Court ruled that a bank guarantee equal to the alleged short-paid GST amount was sufficient for the interim release of a consignment. The Directorate General of GST Intelligence (DGGI) had sought a higher guarantee including penalties, but the court rejected this plea. The ruling clarifies that interim GST relief is meant to secure only the tax amount, not additional penalties. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle consignment releases during disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Court ruled that furnishing a bank guarantee equal to the alleged short-paid GST amount was adequate for interim release of the consignment. DGGI’s plea to include the penalty component in the guarantee amount was rejected. articles.title: Reassessment Quashed as AO Used Section 147 Instead of Mandatory Section 153C
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad quashed a reassessment order issued by the Assessing Officer (AO) because it was based on search material seized from a third party. The AO had used Section 147 instead of the mandatory Section 153C, which requires a specific satisfaction note. The tribunal ruled that the reassessment lacked jurisdiction and was invalid. This decision highlights the procedural requirements for tax reassessments involving third-party materials.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that reassessment based solely on search material seized from a third party must be initiated under Section 153C and not Sections 147/148. The Tribunal quashed the reassessment for lack of jurisdiction and absence of a mandatory satisfaction note. The post Reassessment Quashed as AO Used Section 147 Instead of Mandatory Section 153C appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India aggressively sold dollars via state-run banks to halt the rupee's slide after it hit all-time lows. This pre-open strategy, last used in March, aims to break a negative feedback loop of depreciation, with sustained oil prices and U.S. yields also pressuring the currency.

articles.title: NCLAT says NCLT cannot directly order SFIO Probe; refers matter to Centre
articles.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) ruled that the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) cannot directly order an investigation by the Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO). The NCLAT clarified that such power lies with the Central Government under the Companies Act and referred the matter to the Centre for further action. This decision impacts how corporate fraud cases are handled and sets a legal precedent for tribunal and investigative agency roles.

Chandurkar dismissed an appeal filed by civic authorities, clarifying that statutory compensation rights are inalienable and cannot be surrendered through contractual conditions.

The Court clarified that once a law grants a person the right to compensation, such right cannot be treated as surrendered merely because the landowner agreed to forgo it as a precondition for receiving another statutory benefit or amenity. This economic shift may influence India's trade strategies, energy policies, and diplomatic engagements with Japan and oil-producing regions.

articles.title: Supreme Court Rules Against Repetitive Order VII Rule 11 CPC Applications on Settled Grounds

The ruling clarifies procedural limits to prevent misuse of legal recourse.

articles.title: ITAT Denies Section 54B Exemption for Failure to Prove Actual Agricultural Use
articles.summary: The Nagpur Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) denied a taxpayer's exemption under Section 54B of the Income Tax Act because they failed to prove actual agricultural use of the land. articles.title: Legal Digest | Why corporate guarantees aren't subjected to GST
articles.summary: Issuing a corporate guarantee to a subsidiary without consideration does not involve any flow of monetary benefits and, therefore, does not constitute a taxable service. articles.title: DRP 180526: Ethanol Production in Drought prone areas: A false solution?
articles.summary: From a tonne of sugarcane juice (about 1,000 litres), only 70 litres of ethanol is produced. The process is doubly water-intensive: first, to grow a crop that guzzles vast quantities of water, and then, to expend further energy and resources to extract ethanol from it. With govt policy incentives, India’s ethanol production capacity has increased dramatically — from about 518 crore litres a decade ago to nearly 2,000 crore litres today. However, current demand is only about 1,100 crore litres. I

Heavy rain and rockfalls triggered the incident, prompting rescue operations by the State Disaster Response Force (SDRF) and National Disaster Response Force (NDRF). The event underscores risks to critical infrastructure in hilly regions, particularly during monsoon season.

articles.summary: The Indian government has imposed an export duty on petrol while reducing the levy on diesel and jet fuel exports. The move affects petroleum product exports and aligns with broader energy and fiscal policy adjustments.

The decision has significant implications for public health, animal welfare, and legal frameworks governing stray dogs.

articles.title: Hormuz chaos nears month 3: Oil prices climb as tightened supplies squeeze fuel reserves
articles.summary: Oil prices rose to $105.83 (Brent) and $99.23 (WTI) as supply disruptions from the Iran conflict and falling US crude reserves raised concerns. The post Service PE Not Created as Only Unique Solar Days Count Under India-US DTAA appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: Delhi ITAT ruled that only unique solar days of employee presence, and not cumulative man-days, should be considered for determining Service PE under the India-US DTAA. articles.title: AI Promises Efficiency but the Fashion Industry Needs a Political Reckoning
articles.summary: Fashion for Good's report outlines a vision for an AI-driven fashion supply chain by 2036, emphasizing technical feasibility and operational seriousness. However, it raises unaddressed concerns about labour shifts, legal responsibility, and regulatory gaps that could either drive progress or perpetuate existing systemic failures. The discussion underscores the need for policy frameworks to manage AI's societal and economic impacts in industries like fashion.
articles.raw_summary: Ten years is a long horizon in any industry, but in AI it may be a category error. Fashion for Good's mapping of a 2036 AI-native supply chain is technically grounded and operationally serious. The structural questions it leaves unanswered, around rebound effects, labour transition, legal accountability, and regulatory reach, are the ones that will determine whether the vision it describes produces genuine progress or faster versions of the same systemic failures.

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Addition Because Stamp Duty Value Ignored Illegal Construction
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that stamp duty valuation cannot be blindly applied to properties affected by illegal construction, such as those subject to BBMP demolition proceedings. The tribunal accepted the actual purchase price as the fair market value and deleted the tax addition. This decision clarifies how tax authorities should assess property values in cases involving unauthorized constructions.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT held that stamp duty valuation could not be blindly adopted where the property was affected by BBMP demolition proceedings for unauthorized construction. The Tribunal accepted the actual purchase price as fair market value and deleted the addition. The association is demanding stricter regulations to control the online pharmaceutical market. This issue raises concerns about patient safety and the need for government oversight in the digital healthcare sector.
articles.raw_summary: Chemists association to go on strike on May 20 over unregulated online medicine sale

articles.title: Twisha Sharma death: Bhopal court dismisses plea seeking second postmortem, CM says will write to CBI for probe
articles.summary: A Bhopal court rejected a request for a second postmortem in the death of Twisha Sharma. Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Mohan Yadav announced he will write to the CBI to seek a probe into the matter. The decision follows public and legal scrutiny of the case.
articles.raw_summary: Twisha Sharma death: Bhopal court dismisses plea seeking second postmortem, CM says will write to CBI for probe

articles.title: Seven Irular families rescued from brick kiln in Arani
articles.summary: Seven Irular tribal families were rescued from a brick kiln in Arani, Tamil Nadu, after being forced to live in unhygienic conditions without drinking water or toilet facilities. Revenue officials reported the families were subjected to exploitative labour conditions. The incident highlights issues of bonded labour and tribal rights violations. Activists may demand stricter enforcement of anti-trafficking and labour laws.
articles.raw_summary: Revenue officials said that the rescued families were forced to stay in small huts without proper access to drinking water and no toilet facility

articles.title: CPI(M) warns Vijay in Tamil Nadu: ‘Will rethink support if AIADMK joins Govt,’ says P Shanmugam
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CPI(M) Secretary P Shanmugam stated that the Left parties may reconsider their support for the state government if the AIADMK joins the cabinet. The warning comes amid the inclusion of the VCK party in the government. This reflects potential political instability and its impact on governance in Tamil Nadu.
articles.raw_summary: Tamil Nadu CPI(M) Secretary P Shanmugam has reiterated that the Left parties would reconsider their support to the TVK government if the AIADMK joins the Tamil Nadu Cabinet, while commenting on VCK's inclusion in the cabinet.

articles.title: Andhra govt clears 800 acres for RIL's AI data centre in Vizianagaram district

This land in Vizianagaram district will host a Giga Scale AI Data Centre with a Cable Landing Station. Reliance plans to invest over Rs 1 lakh crore in this project. The government is also providing various incentives to support the mega project.

The crisis disproportionately affects tribal communities, raising concerns about access to basic resources and rights violations. The situation may prompt government intervention or policy discussions on drought management and rural water supply.

This trend may impact India's textile trade policies or export regulations as global standards evolve.

articles.title: Shapewear Finally Stopped Selling Transformation and Started Selling Infrastructure
articles.summary: The shapewear buyer has changed, and the product is catching up. Across the US, Japan, and India, brands are retiring the corrective brief and replacing it with an essentials proposition: breathable, inclusive, designed for daily use, and increasingly dependent on fit technology and supply-chain sophistication to deliver what the new consumer language promises. What looks like a category evolution is, on closer inspection, a category reset.

articles.title: Shiv Sena row: SC irked over irresponsible statements by politicians, warns them to be careful
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed strong displeasure over politicians making irresponsible statements alleging that the court was not hearing the Shiv Sena symbol row case. A bench led by Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi cautioned that such conduct is unacceptable. The court noted that parties had sought dates in the matter, contradicting the allegations. The warning underscores the judiciary's authority and the need for responsible discourse on legal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Friday expressed strong displeasure over "irresponsible statements" of some politicians alleging nonhearing of the Shiv Sena symbol row case in the top court, and cautioned that they should be careful as such conduct can't be accepted. A bench of Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi observed that parties before it had sought dates in the matter and then statements were being made that the apex court was not deciding the case.

articles.title: Downside risks to economy have begun materializing as continued West Asia conflict casts shadow over India's trade outlook: Crisil
articles.summary: The escalating conflict in West Asia is emerging as a major risk for India’s economy, threatening trade flows, increasing import costs, and putting pressure on growth. Economists warn that prolonged geopolitical tensions could slow GDP growth and strain the country’s external finances.

Analysts suggest a breakthrough in talks is unlikely if concessions aren't made.

The police’s urgency suggests a need for clearer protocols or resources in such cases.

articles.title: Explainer-Why India's Tata Sons is facing pressure to list
